name: Acorn Squash Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut squash in half lengthwise and remove seeds. Place squash, cut side down, in a broiler pan. Add hot water to pan to a depth of about 1 inch.
2. Bake at 350°F for 30 minutes.
3. Spoon the pulp from the squash into a serving bowl, reserving the pulp.
4. Place carrots and onion in a saucepan; cover with water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
5. Drain; combine vegetables with the reserved pulp and 1/3 cup water in a blender or food processor. Process for 30 seconds or until smooth. Set aside.
6. Melt butter in a large Dutch oven over low heat. Add flour, salt, and pepper, stirring until smooth. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
7. Gradually add the pureed vegetable mixture, chicken broth, ground nutmeg, paprika, ground allspice, red pepper, half-and-half, and, if desired, 1 1/2 tbsp sherry. Bring to a boil. Cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
8. Stir in the remaining half-and-half. If desired, serve in squash shells on a bed of kale, sprinkled with paprika.
Yield: 8 servings
